en.extract:
The brown-throated sunbird (Anthreptes malacensis), also known as the plain-throated sunbird, is a species of bird in the family Nectariniidae. It is found in a wide range of semi-open habitats in south-east Asia, ranging from Myanmar to the Lesser Sundas and west Philippines. The grey-throated sunbird found in the remaining part of the Philippines is often considered a subspecies of the brown-throated sunbird, but the two differ consistently in measurements and plumage, and there is no evidence of intergradation between them.


Taxonomy
The following subspecies are recognised:

A. m. malacensis (Scopoli, 1786) – mainland Southeast Asia, Sumatra and satellite islands, most of Borneo aside from the north, Java and Bali
A. m. anambae Oberholser, 1917 – Anambas Islands
A. m. erixanthus Oberholser, 1932 – Natuna Islands
A. m. bornensis Riley, 1920 – north Borneo
A. m. mjobergi Bangs & Peters, JL, 1927 – Maratua
A. m. paraguae Riley, 1920 – Palawan
A. m. heliolusius Oberholser, 1923 – Mindanao
A. m. wiglesworthi Hartert, 1902 – Sulu Archipelago
A. m. iris Parkes, 1971 – Sibutu Islands
A. m. chlorigaster Sharpe, 1877 – Western Visayas
A. m. cagayanensis Mearns, 1905 – Mapun
A. m. heliocalus Oberholser, 1923 – Sangihe and Siau
A. m. celebensis Shelley, 1878 – Sulawesi and satellite islands
A. m. extremus Mees, 1966 – Banggai and Sula
A. m. convergens Rensch, 1929 – Lombok to Alor
A. m. rubrigena Rensch, 1931 – Sumba


Description
The brown-throated sunbird is a relatively large, heavy sunbird with a thick bill. Measuring some 14 centimetres (5.5 in) in length, it has a mass of 7.4–13.5 g (0.26–0.48 oz), with males averaging slightly larger than females.
Like most sunbirds, the male brown-throated sunbird is more colourful than the female. The male has iridescent green and purple upperparts with chestnut on the wing-coverts and scapulars; it is primarily yellow below. The female is olive-green above and yellowish below.


Behaviour
The brown-throated sunbird primarily feeds on nectar, but it will also take small fruits and berries. Juveniles are fed with insects.


Gallery


References

Cheke, Robert A., Mann, Clive F., & Allen, Richard. (2001). Sunbirds: A Guide to the Sunbirds, Flowerpeckers, Spiderhunters and Sugarbirds of the World. Christopher Helm, London. pp. 184–186.

The '''brown-throated sunbird''' ('''''Anthreptes malacensis'''''), also known as the '''plain-throated sunbird''', is a species of [[bird]] in the family [[Nectariniidae]]. It is found in a wide range of semi-open habitats in [[south-east Asia]], ranging from [[Myanmar]] to the [[Lesser Sundas]] and west [[Philippines]]. The [[grey-throated sunbird]] found in the remaining part of the Philippines is often considered a [[subspecies]] of the brown-throated sunbird, but the two differ consistently in measurements and [[plumage]], and there is no evidence of [[intergradation]] between them.<ref>Cheke, R. A., & Mann, C. F. (2008). Brown-throated Sunbird (Anthreptes malacensis). pp. 247 in: del Hoyo, J., Elliott, A. & Christie, D. A. eds. (2008). ''Handbook of the Birds of the World.'' Vol. 13. Penduline-tits to Shrikes. Lynx Edicions, Barcelona. {{ISBN|978-84-96553-45-3}}</ref>

== Taxonomy ==
The following [[subspecies]] are recognised:<ref>{{Cite web |title=Dippers, leafbirds, flowerpeckers, sunbirds – IOC World Bird List |url=https://www.worldbirdnames.org/new/bow/dippers/ |access-date=2023-01-28 |website=www.worldbirdnames.org}}</ref>

* ''A. m. malacensis'' <small>([[Giovanni Antonio Scopoli|Scopoli]], 1786)</small> – [[mainland Southeast Asia]], [[Sumatra]] and satellite islands, most of [[Borneo]] aside from the north, [[Java]] and [[Bali]]
* ''A. m. anambae'' <small>[[Harry C. Oberholser|Oberholser]], 1917</small> – [[Anambas Islands Regency|Anambas Islands]]
* ''A. m. erixanthus'' <small>Oberholser, 1932</small> – [[Natuna Regency|Natuna Islands]]
* ''A. m. bornensis'' <small>[[Joseph Harvey Riley|Riley]], 1920</small> – north Borneo
* ''A. m. mjobergi'' <small>[[Outram Bangs|Bangs]] & [[James L. Peters|Peters, JL]], 1927</small> – [[Maratua Island|Maratua]]
* ''A. m. paraguae'' <small>Riley, 1920</small> – [[Palawan]]
* ''A. m. heliolusius'' <small>Oberholser, 1923</small> – [[Mindanao]]
* ''A. m. wiglesworthi'' <small>[[Ernst Hartert|Hartert]], 1902</small> – [[Sulu Archipelago]]
* ''A. m. iris'' <small>[[Kenneth Carroll Parkes|Parkes]], 1971</small> – [[Sibutu Islands]]
* ''A. m. chlorigaster'' <small>[[Richard Bowdler Sharpe|Sharpe]], 1877</small> – [[Western Visayas]]
* ''A. m. cagayanensis'' <small>[[Edgar Alexander Mearns|Mearns]], 1905</small> – [[Mapun]]
* ''A. m. heliocalus'' <small>Oberholser, 1923</small> – [[Sangir Island|Sangihe]] and [[Siau Island|Siau]]
* ''A. m. celebensis'' <small>[[George Ernest Shelley|Shelley]], 1878</small> – [[Sulawesi]] and satellite islands
* ''A. m. extremus'' <small>[[Gerlof Mees|Mees]], 1966</small> – [[Banggai Island|Banggai]] and [[Sula Island|Sula]] 
* ''A. m. convergens'' <small>[[Bernhard Rensch|Rensch]], 1929</small> – [[Lombok]] to [[Alor Island|Alor]]
* ''A. m. rubrigena'' <small>Rensch, 1931</small> – [[Sumba]]

==Description==
The brown-throated sunbird is a relatively large, heavy [[sunbird]] with a thick [[Beak|bill]]. Measuring some {{convert|14|cm|in}} in length, it has a mass of {{convert|7.4|–|13.5|g|oz|abbr=on}}, with males averaging slightly larger than females.

Like most sunbirds, the male brown-throated sunbird is more colourful than the female. The male has iridescent green and purple upperparts with chestnut on the wing-coverts and scapulars; it is primarily yellow below. The female is olive-green above and yellowish below.

==Behaviour==
The brown-throated sunbird primarily feeds on [[nectar]], but it will also take small fruits and berries. [[Juvenile (organism)|Juveniles]] are fed with insects.

zh.extract:
褐喉食蜜鸟（台湾作褐喉直嘴太陽鳥）（学名：Anthreptes malacensis），是雀形目太阳鸟科的一种鸟类。


特征
褐喉食蜜鸟体重约11.33克，翼长约67.1毫米，嘴峰长约20.3毫米，喙宽度约3.7毫米，喙厚度约3.3毫米，跗蹠长约16.4毫米，尾长约39.6毫米。褐喉食蜜鸟在其分布区内为留鸟，其栖息在半开放的中等高度的林地中，食性为杂食性，主要食物来源是陆生无脊椎动物。


亚种
A. m. malacensis：分布于缅甸南部至泰国半岛、印度支那、苏门答腊岛和南婆羅洲島。
A. m. mjobergi：分布于马拉图阿岛。
A. m. borneensis：分布于北婆罗洲（沙巴、文莱和邻近岛屿）。
A. m. chlorigaster：分布于宿务、马斯巴特岛、莱特岛、班乃岛、锡布延岛、塔布拉斯岛、朗布隆岛、蒂考岛。
A. m. heliolusius：分布于菲律宾南部（巴西兰岛、棉兰老岛西部和中部以及Talikud Island）。
A. m. cagayanensis：分布于卡加延苏禄岛（菲律宾南部）。
A. m. paraguae：分布于菲律宾南部（Balabac、库里昂、巴拉望和卡拉依特岛）。
A. m. wiglesworthi：分布于苏禄群岛（邦奥岛、霍洛岛、Tawitawi和Basbas）。
A. m. iris：分布于菲律宾南部（西布图和西坦基）。
A. m. heliocalus：分布于桑义赫岛和锡奥岛（苏拉威西岛附近）。
A. m. celebensis：分布于苏拉威西岛和邻近岛屿。
A. m. extremus：分布于邦盖岛和Sula。
A. m. convergens：分布于西小巽他群岛（龙目岛至潘塔尔岛和阿洛岛）。
A. m. rubrigena：分布于松巴岛（小巽他群岛）。
A. m. anambae：分布于阿南巴斯岛（中国南海）。
